plan.goal: Follow up on remaining PRISM usability gaps by improving compact-tool ranking transparency, strengthening concept-to-doc-to-code continuity, hardening workspace/path edge cases, and tightening stale-context repair loops.
plan.title: Follow up on remaining PRISM usability gaps by improving compact-tool ranking transparency, strengthening concept-to-doc-to-code continuity, hardening workspace/path edge cases, and tightening stale-context repair loops.

graph.title: Follow up on remaining PRISM usability gaps by improving compact-tool ranking transparency, strengthening concept-to-doc-to-code continuity, hardening workspace/path edge cases, and tightening stale-context repair loops.
graph.goal: Follow up on remaining PRISM usability gaps by improving compact-tool ranking transparency, strengthening concept-to-doc-to-code continuity, hardening workspace/path edge cases, and tightening stale-context repair loops.

nodes.title: Explain why compact-tool results won ranking
nodes.summary: Added compact locate ranking transparency by surfacing a top-level selectionReason alongside per-candidate whyShort output, preserving stronger ranked reasons instead of discarding them, covering direct locate plus MCP round-trip behavior with regressions, validating focused locate tests and the full workspace suite, and rebuilding/restarting the release MCP daemon.

acceptance.label: Compact-tool responses expose a concise, trustworthy explanation for why the top result or candidate won.

nodes.title: Strengthen concept to doc to code continuity
nodes.summary: Inspecting concept -> governing doc -> code-owner continuity across compact concept/open/workset flows to reduce manual stitching between those surfaces.

acceptance.label: Common concept flows can carry the user from concept packets into governing docs and then into code owners without manual stitching.

nodes.title: Harden workspace and path edge cases

acceptance.label: Workspace and path-sensitive behavior remains stable across temp roots, canonicalization differences, and parallel test execution.
When PRISM detects stale or detached session context, the user gets a direct repair path rather than explanation only.
